name: La Palma del Condado - Corumbel Reservoir Loop
name_original: Villalba de Alcor y La Palma del Condado – circular por el Embalse de Corumbel
description: This moderate 21.8-mile mountain bike route circles the Embalse de Corumbel, offering diverse landscapes and river views in Huelva.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Villalba Del Alcor offers diverse terrain across the Condado region of Huelva province. The area features a mix of "campiña" (farmland), "alcores" (hills), and "dehesa" (Mediterranean savannas), providing varied landscapes for riders. Extensive vineyards, olive groves, and eucalyptus groves characterize many routes, alongside streams that contribute to the natural corridors. This combination of agricultural scenery and natural features creates a rich environment for mountain bike trails. Best mountain bike trails around Villalba Del Alcor The most popular mountain bike trail is La Palma del Condado - Corumbel Reservoir Loop, a 21.8 miles (35.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 36 minutes to complete. This route offers views of the Corumbel Reservoir and surrounding agricultural lands. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Church of Saint Bartholomew loop from Villalba del Alcor, a moderate 30.1 miles (48.4 km) path. This trail winds through varied landscapes, including olive groves and open fields. Local mountain bikers also love the Corumbel Reservoir – Church of Saint Bartholomew loop from Villalba del Alcor, a 19.7 miles (31.8 km) trail leading through agricultural scenery and near waterways, often completed in about 2 hours 19 minutes. Mountain biking around Villalba Del Alcor is defined by agricultural lands, undulating hills, and dehesa areas. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths through vineyards to more challenging routes in varied terrain. The routes in Villalba Del Alcor are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 700 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Villalba Del Alcor's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Villalba Del Alcor?
A: Mountain biking around Villalba Del Alcor offers a diverse mix of landscapes. You'll encounter 'campiña' (farmland) with extensive vineyards, olive groves, and cereal crops, as well as undulating 'alcores' (hills) and 'dehesa' (Mediterranean savannas). Some routes also feature eucalyptus groves and natural corridors along streams, providing varied surfaces from compacted gravel to sandy or stony forest tracks.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Villalba Del Alcor?
A: There are over 30 mountain bike trails around Villalba Del Alcor, catering to various skill levels. This includes 3 easy routes, 28 moderate routes, and 1 difficult route, ensuring options for most riders.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in the area?
A: Yes, Villalba Del Alcor offers 3 easy mountain bike routes that are suitable for families. These trails often pass through scenic vineyards and agricultural lands, providing a pleasant experience for riders of all ages. Look for routes with lower difficulty ratings for the most family-friendly options.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I might see while mountain biking?
A: Many routes offer glimpses of local landmarks and natural features. You might pass by the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5164288" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Saint Bartholomew&lt;/a&gt;, or ride near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995698466/la-palma-del-condado-corumbel-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corumbel Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. The region is also part of the 'Ruta del Vino Condado de Huelva', so you'll often cycle through picturesque vineyards and olive groves.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Villalba Del Alcor?
A: The mountain bike trails in Villalba Del Alcor are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.7 stars from over 35 reviews. Riders frequently praise the dreamlike landscapes, the variety of terrain, and the immersive experience of cycling through olive trees and traditional dehesa areas.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders looking for a longer challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20119844" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chucena Town Hall – Church of Saint Bartholomew loop from Villalba del Alcor&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 50.6 km (31.4 miles) route that takes nearly 4 hours to complete, offering a substantial ride through varied landscapes.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in Villalba Del Alcor?
A: Many of the mountain bike trails in Villalba Del Alcor are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20118748" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Saint Bartholomew loop from Villalba del Alcor&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 48.4 km (30.1 miles) circular route that winds through diverse scenery.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Villalba Del Alcor?
A: The region's Mediterranean climate generally makes spring and autumn ideal for mountain biking, offering milder temperatures and pleasant conditions. During these seasons, the agricultural landscapes are particularly vibrant, and the weather is comfortable for longer rides.

Q: Are there any routes that combine natural features with agricultural scenery?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40337303" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corumbel Reservoir – Church of Saint Bartholomew loop from Villalba del Alcor&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, leading you through agricultural scenery while also passing near waterways and offering views of the Corumbel Reservoir.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on the trails?
A: Beyond the agricultural fields, you'll encounter eucalyptus groves and traditional 'dehesa' areas with scattered trees. The region is also crisscrossed by streams like Arroyo de los Morantes and Arroyo de la Fuente, which contribute to a rich biodiversity and provide natural corridors for some routes.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the Corumbel Reservoir?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995698466/la-palma-del-condado-corumbel-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Palma del Condado - Corumbel Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a 35.1 km (21.8 miles) trail specifically designed to offer scenic views of the Corumbel Reservoir and the surrounding agricultural lands.

Q: How challenging are the mountain bike trails in Villalba Del Alcor?
A: The trails range from easy to difficult. While most of the 32 routes are rated as moderate, there are 3 easy options for beginners or casual rides, and 1 difficult trail for those seeking a more intense challenge. The varied terrain ensures there's something for every skill level.
tips: It is a church registered in an old Almohad fortress that was declared an Asset of Cultural Interest in 1931. It is undoubtedly the most outstanding monument in Villalba del Alcor.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a moderate mountain bike tour. It's suitable for riders with good physical fitness and some cycling experience. According to Komoot's difficulty levels, a moderate trail like this might involve up to 5 hours of riding and up to 900 meters of positive elevation gain, corresponding to an STS S2 technical difficulty.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the route?
faqs.answer: You can expect a variety of terrains on this route. While specific details for the entire circular route are not fully outlined, the general area often includes dirt roads. The presence of the Embalse de Corumbel (Corumbel Reservoir) suggests trails along its perimeter. Some sections might be relatively smooth and easy, while others could present more challenge.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: Riders can anticipate picturesque and varied scenery. The trail is designed to encompass several biotopes and environments, including riverbanks, gallery forests, and areas offering panoramic views. You'll see natural elements like the reservoir itself, and potentially hermitages. The starting point of Villalba del Alcor is surrounded by extensive fields of vineyards and cereals, adding to the agricultural charm of the region.

faqs.question: What notable landmarks or natural features will I see on the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers several points of interest. You'll pass by the historic Church of Saint Bartholomew and the charming Plaza de España in La Palma del Condado. A significant natural feature is the Crossing of the Tamujoso and Corumbel Rivers, and the trail also circles the scenic Embalse de Corumbel.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: While specific seasonal conditions are not detailed, considering the Huelva province's climate, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for mountain biking. Summers can be very hot, and winters might be cooler and wetter. Always check local weather forecasts before you go.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: The route is a circular one starting and ending in the Villalba de Alcor area. While specific parking lots aren't mentioned, you would typically look for public parking within Villalba de Alcor or La Palma del Condado, which are the main towns associated with the trail. Ensure you park responsibly and legally.

faqs.question: Is public transport available to reach the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Information on specific public transport links directly to the trailhead is not available. However, Villalba de Alcor and La Palma del Condado are towns in Huelva province, so there may be regional bus services connecting them to larger cities. It's advisable to check local bus schedules if you plan to use public transport.

faqs.question: Is the route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, trails in natural areas are dog-friendly, but it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially around livestock, wildlife, or other trail users. Be prepared to carry out any waste. Specific regulations for this particular route are not provided, so common courtesy and local guidelines should be followed.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that a permit or entrance fee is required to access this mountain bike route. It appears to be freely accessible for public use.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation nearby?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through or near Villalba de Alcor and La Palma del Condado, you can expect to find amenities such as cafes, pubs, restaurants, and accommodation options in these towns. These would be ideal spots for refreshments or an overnight stay before or after your ride.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other paths. You will ride along parts of the Camino de los Serranos, Camino Molino Nuevo, Plaza de la Magdalena, Colada Padrón de los Carboneros, and Camino de la Pastora, among others.
